cnbc.title: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks
cnbc.summary: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their ceasefire by three weeks following a meeting in the White House with top U.S. officials, President Donald Trump said.

cnbc.title: U.S. soldier arrested for $400K winning Polymarket bets on Maduro capture, DOJ says
cnbc.summary: The arrest comes as concerns have grown about people with inside information making bets on the Polymarket and Kalshi prediction markets.

cnbc.title: Microsoft plans first-ever voluntary employee buyout for up to 7% of U.S. workforce
cnbc.summary: Microsoft's inaugural voluntary buyouts will be open to workers at the senior director level and below whose years of employment and age add up to 70 or more.

cnbc.title: Meta will cut 10% of workforce as company pushes deeper into AI
cnbc.summary: Meta plans to lay off 10% of its workforce, about 8,000 employees, as the company continues to ramp up investments in artificial intelligence.

cnbc.title: Regeneron inks drug pricing deal with Trump, will offer new hearing-loss therapy for free
cnbc.summary: Regeneron is the latest in a string of major drugmakers to make pricing concessions for new and existing medicines under agreements with Trump.

cnbc.title: Spirit Airlines lawyer says cash 'not going to last for very much longer,' but government rescue on the table
cnbc.summary: Spirit Airlines confirmed it is in talks with the Trump administration for a rescue package.

cnbc.title: Japan core inflation accelerates after five months as Iran war pushes energy prices higher
cnbc.summary: Government data showed the inflation figure — which strips out prices of fresh food — was in line with the 1.8% expected by economists polled by Reuters.

cnbc.title: Intel's stock soars 20% as results top estimates, with chipmaker showing signs of growth
cnbc.summary: Intel has been a Wall Street darling of late even as the business has yet to find much momentum.

cnbc.title: Nike cuts 1,400 roles in second round of layoffs this year
cnbc.summary: Nike announced 1,400 layoffs, primarily in its technology department, on Thursday after cutting 775 jobs in January.

cnbc.title: These charts show how Iran's economy is in freefall
cnbc.summary: Tehran's hardline rhetoric belies a brutal reality facing the regime and Iranian citizens: its economy, vulnerable even before the war, is now in tatters.
